description:
**Problem**
Test harness currently relies on monolithic internal paths that block extraction.
design: Re-home daemon-internal fixtures/harness to daemon-owned module boundaries while preserving deterministic tests.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Progress: moved repl proto schema/codec ownership into crates/beads-daemon-core (new repl::proto module) and converted beads-rs daemon repl/proto.rs into a compatibility re-export shim. This reduces fixture dependence on daemon internals and sets up direct daemon-core imports for harness modules.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Additional progress: integration fixtures repl_frames.rs and repl_transport.rs now import frame/proto types from beads-daemon-core instead of beads-rs daemon internals. Validated with targeted tests fixtures_repl_frames_roundtrip and fixtures_repl_transport_roundtrip.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: More progress: daemon-core now owns repl::proto (beads-rs proto module is a re-export shim), and fixture imports in repl_frames/repl_transport now target beads-daemon-core directly. Added daemon-core test coverage validation: cargo test -p beads-daemon-core passes (44 tests).

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Progress: moved additional integration fixture ownership toward daemon/surface boundaries. Updates include switching fixture IPC+op imports to beads_rs::surface, consuming shared polling/socket helpers from beads_daemon::test_utils, and removing fixtures/daemon_test_utils.rs shim. Verified with cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration -- daemon:: (16 pass), -- repl:: (9 pass), and -- wal::idempotency:: (2 pass). Remaining blockers are explicit missing typed boundary APIs for deep wal/store_lock/repl session internals still imported by a subset of fixtures.

- Store-lock fixture decoupled from daemon::store_lock internals.

- cargo check -p beads-rs
Acceptance status: harness+fixtures compile and integration suites remain green with no new global-state coupling introduced.

description:
**Problem**
The daemon runtime (~20k lines after WAL extraction) still lives in \`beads-rs/src/daemon/\`. beads-rs is supposed to be a thin facade but it's the largest crate by 2x. Agents can reach into daemon internals through \`beads_rs::daemon::*\` because there's no crate boundary — only module-level \`pub(crate)\`.

**Design**
Move the remaining daemon implementation from \`beads-rs/src/daemon/\` into \`beads-daemon\`.

Layout inside beads-daemon after move:
\`\`\`
crates/beads-daemon/src/
├── runtime/               ← NEW: from beads-rs/src/daemon/
│   ├── core/              ← Daemon struct, loaded stores, scheduling
│   ├── server/            ← Unix socket, state loop, dispatch
│   ├── repl/              ← repl session orchestration
│   ├── store/             ← discovery, locking, runtime
│   ├── executor.rs        ← mutation execution pipeline
│   ├── mutation_engine.rs ← pure mutation planning
│   ├── query_executor.rs  ← read path
│   ├── admin.rs           ← admin operations
│   ├── scrubber.rs        ← GC / maintenance
│   ├── git_worker.rs      ← git sync bridge
│   ├── git_backend.rs     ← git2 adapter
│   ├── subscription.rs    ← realtime subscriptions
│   ├── ops.rs             ← error types
│   ├── coord.rs           ← coordination
│   ├── durability_coordinator.rs
│   ├── checkpoint_scheduler.rs
│   ├── export_worker.rs
│   ├── fingerprint.rs
│   ├── metrics.rs
│   └── ...
├── admission.rs           ← existing
├── broadcast.rs           ← existing
├── clock.rs               ← existing
├── remote.rs              ← existing
├── scheduler.rs           ← existing
├── layout.rs              ← from bd-6ea1.2
└── lib.rs
\`\`\`

Visibility: \`runtime\` internals are \`pub(crate)\`. Only \`run_daemon()\`, \`DaemonLayout\`, \`DaemonRuntimeConfig\`, and testkit are public.

Import rewrites inside moved code:
- \`crate::core::*\` → \`beads_core::*\`
- \`crate::git::*\` → \`beads_git::*\`
- \`crate::daemon::wal::*\` → \`beads_daemon_core::wal::*\`
- \`crate::paths::*\` → use threaded \`DaemonLayout\`
- \`crate::config::*\` → use threaded \`DaemonRuntimeConfig\`

New deps for beads-daemon Cargo.toml:
\`\`\`toml
beads-daemon-core = { path = "../beads-daemon-core", features = ["wal-sqlite", "wal-fs"] }
beads-git = { path = "../beads-git" }
git2 = { version = "0.20", features = ["vendored-libgit2", "vendored-openssl"] }
crossbeam = "0.8"
signal-hook = "0.3"
rusqlite = "0.31"
# ... other deps currently in beads-rs
\`\`\`

**Acceptance**
- [ ] All daemon runtime modules moved to \`beads-daemon/src/runtime/\`
- [ ] \`beads-daemon\` runtime internals are \`pub(crate)\`
- [ ] Only \`run_daemon()\`, layout/config types, and testkit are public
- [ ] \`beads-rs/src/daemon/\` deleted or reduced to thin compat shim
- [ ] \`beads-rs::run_daemon_command()\` calls \`beads_daemon::run_daemon()\`
- [ ] \`docs/CRATE_DAG.md\` updated with new edges
- [ ] \`cargo check --workspace\` passes
- [ ] \`cargo test --workspace\` passes  
- [ ] \`just dylint\` passes
- [ ] IPC behavior unchanged

**Files:** \`crates/beads-daemon/src/\` (massive expansion), \`crates/beads-daemon/Cargo.toml\`, \`crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/\` (delete or shim), \`crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs\`, \`docs/CRATE_DAG.md\`

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
**CRITICAL — additional coupling points NOT in the import rewrite table:**

The import rewrite list in the description covers the main paths but misses these:

1. **`crate::compat` coupling** — `daemon/export_worker.rs` and `daemon/core/mod.rs` import:
   ```
   use crate::compat::{ExportContext, ensure_symlinks, export_jsonl};
   use crate::compat::ExportContext;
   ```
   These are JSONL export compatibility functions that stay in beads-rs. Options:
   - Move `ExportContext` + `export_jsonl` + `ensure_symlinks` into beads-daemon (preferred if they don't have other beads-rs deps)
   - Or inject via a trait on the export worker

2. **`crate::telemetry::schema` coupling** — `daemon/executor.rs` and `daemon/server/tests.rs` import:
   ```
   use crate::telemetry::schema;
   ```
   Telemetry stays in beads-rs. Options:
   - Move the schema module to beads-daemon (it's probably just span/field name constants)
   - Or eliminate it if it's only used in tests

3. **`crate::error::{Effect, Transience}`** — used in `daemon/ops.rs`. These are just re-exports from beads-core, so change to `beads_core::{Effect, Transience}`. Easy.

4. **Internal `super::*` and `crate::daemon::*` paths** — all daemon code uses these extensively. When moved to `beads-daemon/src/runtime/`, `crate::daemon::FOO` becomes `crate::runtime::FOO` and `super::` mostly stays the same if you preserve the module tree shape.

5. **`model/` module** — `crates/beads-rs/src/model/` has coupling to daemon (see non-cli-daemon-coupling-inventory.md). Decide: does model/ move to beads-daemon, beads-daemon-core, or stay in beads-rs?

Recommend the implementing agent run `rg "crate::compat|crate::telemetry|crate::error|crate::model|crate::store_admin|crate::repo" crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/` before starting to get the full picture.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
**Scope & approach note:**

This bead moves 68 .rs files (after WAL extraction). It's the largest single task in the epic — expect 2-3 days of focused work.

Recommended approach:
1. Create the `runtime/` module structure in beads-daemon first (empty mod.rs files)
2. Move files in batches by subsystem: store/ → repl/ → server/ → core/ → remaining top-level
3. After each batch, fix `crate::daemon::*` → `crate::runtime::*` paths within moved code
4. Fix external imports (`crate::core::*` → `beads_core::*`, `crate::git::*` → `beads_git::*`, etc.)
5. `cargo check` after each batch to catch issues incrementally
6. Don't try to fix tests in this bead — that's bd-6ea1.7

The move is mechanical but the internal `super::*` references mean you can't easily do it file-by-file — subsystems import each other. Batch by directory.

title: Move daemon runtime into beads-daemon crate

acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Test: label dot collision resolved deterministically, order-independent.\n- [ ] Test: dep dot collision resolved deterministically, order-independent.
description:
**Problem**
No integration-level tests assert deterministic resolution when the same dot is used for different label/dep values.

**Files**: src/core/orset.rs, src/core/state.rs, tests/integration/core/apply.rs
design: **Design**\nAdd tests for label/dep dot collisions using identical dots but different values, assert deterministic winner (lexicographic then hash tie-break) and order-independence.
title: OR-Set dot collision tests for labels/deps
